attributes.description: Alerts when thread pool utilisation exceeds 80% (`currentThreadsBusy / maxThreads > 0.80`). This is the primary saturation signal for Tomcat; requests begin queuing and refusing here.
attributes.name: [Tomcat OTel] Thread pool saturation

investigation_guide.blob:
## Tomcat thread pool saturation

### What fired
`currentThreadsBusy / maxThreads` on a connector exceeded 80% during the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
Thread pool utilisation is the single most important saturation indicator in Tomcat. Above 80%, bursts begin to queue; at 100% the accept queue (`acceptCount`) absorbs further connections until it too is full, after which new connections are refused at the TCP level.

### Triage
1. Thread Pool & Connections dashboard: confirm per-connector utilisation trend.
2. Check request latency (`processingTime / requestCount`) — slow requests pin threads longer.
3. Inspect downstream dependency health (DB, HTTP clients) to find the source of the slowness.
4. Look at connection count and keep-alive count — unhealthy keep-alive patterns inflate usage.

### Remediation
- Fix the slow downstream so threads are released faster.
- Raise `maxThreads` (verify heap and CPU can support it).
- Scale Tomcat horizontally behind a load balancer.

### Tuning
- `> 80%` matches documented guidance. Tighten to 70% for latency-sensitive tiers; 85% is the absolute ceiling before the rule becomes late to warn.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-tomcat.otel-*
| WHERE Catalina_ThreadPool_currentThreadsBusy IS NOT NULL
    AND Catalina_ThreadPool_maxThreads IS NOT NULL
| STATS
    busy = MAX(Catalina_ThreadPool_currentThreadsBusy),
    max_threads = MAX(Catalina_ThreadPool_maxThreads)
  BY host.name, attributes.name
| WHERE max_threads > 0
| EVAL utilization_pct = ROUND(busy / max_threads * 100.0, 2)
// Thread pool saturation threshold — adjust based on your capacity requirements
// Above 80% the risk of request queuing and rejection increases rapidly
| WHERE utilization_pct > 80.0
| SORT utilization_pct DESC
